item.name: Demo Scenarios
item.description:
Three demo scenarios matching the README data flow cases.

The vector DB stores ONLY campaign descriptions (company-specific data).
Generic business definitions (ROI, enrollment rate) come from the LLM.
Performance analysis is computed by the LLM from raw SQL data.

1. **Case 1: Definition Only** — LLM provides the definition; RAG finds only weak campaign description matches.
2. **Case 2: Simple Data Question** — LLM knows the definition + SQL provides data + RAG provides campaign context.
3. **Case 3: Complex Report** — SQL provides raw data + RAG provides campaign description + LLM computes metrics and writes the narrative.

item.name: Case 1: Definition Only (Partial RAG + LLM)
item.description:
The question asks for a general definition of 'enrollment' and its types.
The vector DB only has campaign descriptions — NO glossary, NO definitions.
RAG returns weak matches (campaign texts that mention enrollment).
Claude provides the actual definition from its own trained knowledge.

Run 1a to see the full agent answer, then 1b to verify RAG only has
campaign descriptions (weak matches with high distances >1.0).

item.name: 1a. Ask: What is enrollment and its types? (agent answer)
1b. Search: What RAG actually has about enrollment (raw chunks)
Case 2: Simple Data Question (RAG + SQL + LLM)
item.description:
A compound question: definition (LLM knowledge) + data (SQL) + campaign context (RAG).

The agent uses its own knowledge for 'enrollment rate' definition,
calls sql_query_tool to find which campaign has the most enrollments,
and optionally calls rag_search_tool for campaign description context.

Run 2a for the definition, 2b for data only, 2c for both combined.

item.name: 2a. Ask: What is enrollment rate? (definition via LLM knowledge)
2b. Ask SQL: Which campaign has the highest enrollment? (data via SQL)
2c. Ask: Combined — definition + data in one question (agent uses both tools)
Case 3: Complex Report Request (RAG + SQL + LLM)
item.description:
The most complex flow using performance_summary_tool.

CMP-003 (Spring Dining Deal) is used because it has the best story:
highest redemption rate, student-focused, smallest budget ($100K)
yet highest ROI, mobile-dominant channel.

The tool internally runs 3 SQL queries (raw data) + 1 RAG search
(campaign description), then sends everything to Claude. Claude
computes derived metrics (redemption rate, cost-per-enrollment)
from the raw numbers and writes the narrative report.

Run 3a-3c to see the individual pieces, then 3d for the full report.

item.name: 3a. Get: Campaign details for CMP-003 (raw data)
3b. Search: RAG context for CMP-003 (knowledge base chunks)
3c. Ask SQL: Monthly performance trend for CMP-003 (raw SQL data)
3d. Get Summary: Full AI-generated performance report for CMP-003
